verse_text: This is your lot and your portion for your disobedience against Me,' says the Lord, 'because you forgot Me and trusted in falsehoods.

links.context: The great harlot is Rome, the empire with its claims of divinity, sovereignty, and eternity (see vv. 9, 18), opposed to God and the Church: symbolic of all evil rule. In the Bible, harlotry frequently symbolizes apostasy and idolatry (see Is 1:21; 23:15; [[JER.13:25]]- 27; Ezk 16; [[HOS.4:12]]; [[NAH.3:4]]). The many waters on which the harlot is seated portray both the waters of Babylon--the canals and tributaries of the Euphrates ([[PSA.136:1]]; Jer 28:12, 13)--and the waters of Rome, the seas surrounding Italy. They symbolize the nations who submit to the harlot's rule (v. 15).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
